prompt_text: Suggest a fitness plan to build muscle for someone who has no access to gym equipment.
response_text:
This is a great goal, and you can absolutely build significant muscle with just your bodyweight. The key is **progressive overload**—making the exercise harder over time—since you can't just add more weight on a barbell.

Here is a comprehensive, equipment-free fitness plan designed for muscle growth (hypertrophy).

### The Core Principles for Bodyweight Muscle Growth

1.  **Volume & Intensity:** You need to take each set close to failure (0-2 reps left in the tank). Bodyweight exercises are often easier, so you must push hard.
2.  **Progressive Overload (The "How to Make it Harder" List):**
    - **Increase Reps:** Go from 10 reps to 12, then 15, then 20.
    - **Decrease Rest:** Cut rest from 90 seconds to 60, then 45.
    - **Change Leverage:** Make the movement mechanically harder (e.g., Push-ups from incline -> flat -> decline -> pike).
    - **Add Tempo:** Slow down the lowering phase (e.g., 3 seconds down, 1 second up).
    - **Reduce Stability:** Single-leg versions (e.g., Pistol squats vs. bodyweight squats).
    - **Increase Time Under Tension (TUT):** Pause at the hardest part of the movement.
3.  **Frequency:** Hit each major muscle group 2-3 times per week.
4.  **Recovery:** Muscle is built when you rest, not when you work out. Get 7-9 hours of sleep and eat enough protein.

---

### The Plan: 3-Day Full Body Split

This is the most effective approach for a beginner/intermediate with no equipment. It hits every major muscle group three times a week.

**Schedule:** Workout A (Mon), Rest (Tue), Workout B (Wed), Rest (Thu), Workout C (Fri), Rest (Sat & Sun).

---

### Workout A (Strength & Push Focus)

| Exercise | Sets | Reps | Notes & Progression Tips |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Incline Push-ups** (or Pike Push-ups) | 3 | 8-15 | **Target:** Upper chest & shoulders. Start with hands on a chair/table. Progress to flat, then decline (feet on chair). |
| **2. Bodyweight Squats** (or Split Squats) | 3 | 15-25 | **Target:** Quads & Glutes. For harder: Bulgarian Split Squats (rear foot on chair). |
| **3. Inverted Rows** (under a sturdy table) | 3 | 6-12 | **Target:** Back & Biceps. If you have no table, do **Doorframe Rows** (grip the frame and lean back). Progress by lowering your body angle. |
| **4. Plank** | 3 | 30-60 sec | **Target:** Core. Progress to Side Plank or Plank with shoulder taps. |
| **5. Calf Raises** (on a step/curb) | 3 | 20-30 | **Target:** Calves. Go slow, full range of motion. Single-leg for harder. |

### Workout B (Pull & Legs Focus)

| Exercise | Sets | Reps | Notes & Progression Tips |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Wide Push-ups** | 3 | 10-20 | **Target:** Chest & Triceps (wider grip). |
| **2. Reverse Lunges** (each leg) | 3 | 10-15 per leg | **Target:** Glutes, Quads, Hamstrings. Great for balance. |
| **3. Table Rows** (or Doorframe Rows) | 3 | 8-15 | **Target:** Back & Biceps. Use a low table (you lie under it and pull yourself up). |
| **4. Glute Bridge** (Single-leg) | 3 | 12-15 per leg | **Target:** Glutes & Hamstrings. Lift hips high, squeeze glutes. |
| **5. Bicycle Crunches** | 3 | 15-20 per side | **Target:** Obliques & full core. |

### Workout C (Full Body & Intensity)

| Exercise | Sets | Reps | Notes & Progression Tips |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1. Diamond Push-ups** | 3 | 6-12 | **Target:** Triceps & inner chest. Very hard. Do knee push-ups if needed. |
| **2. Bulgarian Split Squats** | 3 | 8-12 per leg | **Target:** Quad & Glute dominant. The "king" of bodyweight leg exercises. Hold dumbbells (water jugs) for weight. |
| **3. Superman Holds** | 3 | 15-30 sec | **Target:** Lower back & rear shoulders. Lift arms & legs off ground simultaneously. |
| **4. Leg Raises** (Hanging or Lying) | 3 | 10-15 | **Target:** Lower abs. Lying is easier; hanging from a bar is hardest. |
| **5. Burpees** (No Jump) | 3 | 8-12 | **Target:** Full body, cardio & endurance. Step back, not jump, to save joints. |

---

### How to Progress (The 4-Week Cycle)

- **Week 1:** Master the form. Aim for the lower end of the rep ranges.
- **Week 2:** Try to hit the upper end of the rep ranges for all sets.
- **Week 3:** If you can easily hit the upper rep range on the last set, **make the exercise harder** (e.g., switch from incline to flat push-ups, or add a 3-second pause at the bottom).
- **Week 4:** Go for a personal record. Try to beat your best reps from Week 2 on the harder version.

### Crucial Nutrition for Muscle Growth

Without a caloric surplus, you will not build significant muscle.

1.  **Eat in a Calorie Surplus:** Add 200-500 calories to your maintenance level. Track for 2 weeks. If you aren't gaining ~0.5-1 lb per week, eat more.
2.  **Prioritize Protein:** Aim for **1.6 - 2.2 grams of protein per kg of bodyweight** (e.g., a 70kg person needs 112-154g of protein daily).
    - *Sources:* Eggs, Greek yogurt, milk, cottage cheese, lentils, beans, chickpeas, tofu, edamame, peanut butter, chicken/fish (if available).
3.  **Don't Fear Carbs & Fats:** Carbs fuel your workouts. Fats support hormone production (testosterone). Eat whole foods: oats, rice, potatoes, fruits, nuts, avocados.
